content:
type: DEFER
files: Tests/test_imageops.py
spec: Please add tests for transform_callback param to exif_transpose in test_imageops.py. Spec: exif_transpose(image, transform_callback=None, in_place=False). transform_callback is an optional callable taking the orientation int (1-8, from EXIF Orientation tag) and returning either a single Image.Transpose method (or list/sequence of methods) to apply in order, or None to skip transposition for that orientation. When transform_callback is provided, it completely overrides the default orientation-to-method mapping (the dict currently in exif_transpose). When not provided (default None), behavior is unchanged (original dict-based mapping). Please add test cases: (1) test_exif_transpose_transform_callback_basic - use an image with Orientation=6, pass a callback returning Image.Transpose.ROTATE_270 (matching default), assert output equals default exif_transpose output; (2) test_exif_transpose_transform_callback_sequence - callback returns a list of two methods e.g. [Image.Transpose.FLIP_LEFT_RIGHT, Image.Transpose.ROTATE_180] for some orientation, assert result equals manually chaining transpose() calls with both methods in order; (3) test_exif_transpose_transform_callback_none - callback returns None for a given orientation, assert no transposition happens but exif orientation tag is still removed; (4) test that callback receives correct orientation int argument (use a mock/capture). Use images at Tests/images/g/g_*.jpg (orientation test images used elsewhere in this file) as fixtures, consistent with existing tests in the file.

When transform_callback is provided, it completely overrides the default orientation-to-method mapping (the dict currently in exif_transpose). When not provided (default None), behavior is unchanged (original dict-based mapping). Please add test cases: (1) test_exif_transpose_transform_callback_basic - use an image with Orientation=6, pass a callback returning Image.Transpose.ROTATE_270 (matching default), assert output equals default exif_transpose output; (2) test_exif_transpose_transform_callback_sequence - callback returns a list of two methods e.g. [Image.Transpose.FLIP_LEFT_RIGHT, Image.Transpose.ROTATE_180] for some orientation, assert result equals manually chaining transpose() calls with both methods in order; (3) test_exif_transpose_transform_callback_none - callback returns None for a given orientation, assert no transposition happens but exif orientation tag is still removed; (4) test that callback receives correct orientation int argument (use a mock/capture). Use images at Tests/images/g/g_*.jpg (orientation test images used elsewhere in this file) as fixtures, consistent with existing tests in the file.
